description: Tasks that can execute concurrently without resource conflict or shared modifications. Most useful within waves.

groups.name: W-1 sales-blocker parallel set
groups.note: All Wave 1 tasks are independent and can run concurrently. With 3 engineers + 0.5 day each, all 10 complete in 2-3 days. Single-engineer sequential = 4-5 days.

groups.name: W-2 comms infrastructure parallel set
groups.note: ses-proxy Lambda deploy (T2-2) is independent ops work. Plaintext multipart (T2-3) and List-Unsubscribe (T2-4) can batch together (both touch sendViaSes). Soft-bounce (T2-5) is webhook-side, independent. SMS dispatch (T2-1) is independent of email work.

groups.name: W-2 developer platform parallel set
groups.note: TS SDK + Python SDK publish + rate limit policy are pure-CI / pure-policy work. Outbound webhooks (T9-3) is independent of SDK publishing.

groups.name: W-2 substrate honesty parallel set
groups.note: Tier writer (T10-1) + cross-check (T10-2) + atlasVersion (T10-9) are independent. T10-2 sequenced after T10-1 + T10-3.

groups.name: W-2 launch enablement broad
groups.note: AN OSDI sync (T1-3) is independent. A/B winner picker (T1-6) is independent. Workflow verbs (T1-9) depends on T2-2 but can be developed in parallel.

groups.name: W-3 mainnet deployment parallel set
groups.note: These three deploy together in a single mainnet deployment session via combined script execution. Treat as one ops event.

groups.name: W-3 reach extension parallel set
groups.note: State legislator + special district data + country wirings + TIGER monitoring + postal bubble disambiguation can all parallelize. Country rep-lookups (T3-3/4/5) wait for their respective country hydration.

groups.name: W-3 agentic stack parallel set
groups.note: Agentic stack composes; T4-2 + T4-7 can develop in parallel with T4-1 since they share draftedMessages schema. T4-9 (UI) develops in parallel against stubs.

groups.name: W-3 quality signaling parallel set
groups.note: Most Sepolia-side quality work parallelizes; TEE (T5-3) is independent infra track.

groups.name: W-3 accountability parallel set
groups.note: Mainnet anchor (T6-2) + pipeline (T6-1) are sequential. Constituent receipt access + receipt API + browser verification can parallel.

groups.name: W-3 composability parallel set
groups.note: All build on T7-1 once it lands; T7-4 (schema) is independent and can ship in parallel with T7-1.

groups.name: W-3 reader-side parallel set
groups.note: /v/[hash] enhancements + in-browser verify + offline-verify text + privacy model can all proceed in parallel.

groups.name: W-4 phase 3 differentiators parallel set
groups.note: Wave 4 splits into 4 independent threads: agentic delegation (T4-3/4), TEE AI (T5-4), reader-side (T8-1a/1b/5/7/8/9), and continuing composability + reach. Each thread is internally sequenced.

groups.name: Cross-wave: TEE deployment independent infra
groups.note: TEE deployment (T5-3) is independent of all code work; ops elapsed time (2-3 weeks) runs concurrently with software waves. T5-4 (W-4) depends on T5-3 completion.
